108865.explanation: GBSV finds it very important that the municipal council and the Executive Board have an equal number of men and women. We strive for this and hope that many women will also become active in politics.

108866.explanation: GBSV believes that residents should be seriously involved in major decisions. A referendum can help, provided it is used carefully and well explained. It is a supplement to village discussions and other forms of participation.

108868.explanation: GBSV believes permit fees must be proportional to the costs. An increase should not be an end in itself. We want to keep permits affordable for residents and entrepreneurs.

108870.explanation: GBSV believes that a large-scale asylum seeker center does not automatically suit our municipality. We choose tailored and careful decisions, considering livability, support, and available facilities.

108871.explanation: GBSV sees housing for seasonal workers as a shared responsibility of companies and government. We want to prevent work and living becoming too dependent on each other. Tailored solutions are needed.

108873.explanation: GBSV believes that rules about this should be established nationally. The municipality focuses on information provision and addressing concrete nuisance, not on a local ban.

108874.explanation: GBSV sees no added value in a municipal ban on terrace heating. We choose tailored solutions, consultation, and encouraging efficient alternatives.

108875.explanation: GBSV does not see organizing a fireworks show as a core task of the municipality. We focus on safety and providing space for initiatives from residents and associations.

108877.explanation: GBSV believes noise standards must be clear and enforceable. They should be tightened where necessary, with room for tailored approaches so that livability and a vibrant village life remain balanced.

108880.explanation: Sport is important for health and wellbeing. GBSV believes it is important that everyone can participate in sports, even if money is a barrier. Support should be possible.

108881.explanation: GBSV believes the municipality must fulfill its legal obligations towards the local broadcaster. Additional financial support beyond that is not a municipal task.

108883.explanation: GBSV believes it is important that children can also develop through music. Where possible, we support music lessons in cooperation with harmonies and music clubs.

108884.explanation: GBSV considers sports clubs very important. If clubs temporarily run into trouble, the municipality can step in with clear agreements and focused on future sustainability.

108885.explanation: GBSV believes that truly valuable monuments must be preserved carefully. However, monument policy should be evaluated to make clear choices.

108886.explanation: GBSV believes residents must feel safe. Additional community enforcement officers can contribute to visibility and enforcement, with a good balance between effectiveness and costs.

108887.explanation: GBSV is cautious with camera surveillance. It can create a false sense of security. Only in exceptional situations should targeted use be considered.

108888.explanation: GBSV finds protection of victims essential but sees this as a regional service. Cooperation and good referrals are more effective here.

108890.explanation: GBSV wants suitable housing for every life phase. Increasing small homes for the elderly promotes flow and livability and is good for both elderly and other people looking for housing.

108891.explanation: GBSV believes holiday homes are intended for recreation. For non-vital parks, tailored solutions should be possible, where living can be one of the options.

108892.explanation: GBSV manages landscape and space carefully. New construction outside village boundaries is undesirable but sometimes necessary. This requires tailored solutions per location.

108893.explanation: GBSV finds participation important. Socially meaningful work can help, provided it suits a person’s situation and is never imposed without customization.

108895.explanation: GBSV believes that support should align with practical needs. Raising the income threshold can help ensure that people just above the minimum are not left out.

108897.explanation: GBSV does not want automatic expansion or rejection. We assess what is needed per location, considering parking space and the development of electric driving.

108899.explanation: GBSV finds it important that care suits the individual. Where possible, consideration should be given to religion and culture, within the limits of availability and affordability.

108900.explanation: GBSV believes that quality and appropriate care must come first. Price is important but should never be decisive.

108904.explanation: GBSV does not see adding more business parks as a goal in itself. We first look at better use of existing sites.

108906.explanation: GBSV believes social rental homes should be allocated fairly and carefully. We want to do justice to people with residence permits as well as other home seekers who often have been waiting a long time. Therefore, we choose customized solutions within clear rules.

108907.explanation: GBSV wants to protect agricultural land. Solar panels should, in principle, be placed on roofs and existing buildings.

108908.explanation: GBSV is not in favor of spending municipal money on building a new swimming pool at Alphensebaan. The costs are high.

108909.explanation: GBSV considers learning to swim important but does not see school swimming as a structural municipal task that should be funded by municipal money. Parents and schools have their own responsibility here.

108910.explanation: GBSV does not want to interfere substantively with schools' education programs. We are happy to share what characterizes our municipality, such as local culture and history. The Allefs dialect can be part of that.

108911.explanation: GBSV sees no necessity to establish a new coffeeshop. This does not fit with the scale and livability of our municipality.

108912.explanation: GBSV opts for balanced housing development with at least 25% social rental housing and sufficient affordable homes besides that.

108916.explanation: Wind turbines are currently not a concrete issue in Alphen-Chaam. GBSV therefore sees no reason to actively push the province on this. If it comes up, a careful consideration will follow.

108917.explanation: GBSV wants municipal services to be close and accessible. Every village should have a municipal counter or counter function where residents can go with questions, appropriate to the scale of the village.

108865.explanation: The CDA aims for a competent executive board. When suitability is equal, other considerations will play a role, including a balanced representation of society.

108866.explanation: We are not in favor of referenda. For good decision-making, it is important that stakeholders can participate early in the decision-making process. The final decision then lies with the municipal administration.

108868.explanation: When determining specific levies, the CDA focuses on strengthening facilities and community spirit, keeping charges as low as possible and ensuring a fair distribution.

108870.explanation: We take responsibility for refugee shelter under the dispersion law and focus on decent shelter for refugees and displaced persons. Currently, shelter has been arranged for Ukrainians.

108871.explanation: The housing of labor migrants must be appropriate, safe, and of good quality in line with local supply and demand. The municipality has a facilitating and supervisory role regarding the housing of labor migrants. Employers are primarily responsible.

108873.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure. People should be able to arrange their own home environment. However, action must be taken against nuisance.

108874.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure.

108875.explanation: The CDA wants to preserve traditions, even if there is a private fireworks ban. The municipality only has a facilitating role here. Well-organized initiatives by, for example, associations will be supported. Carbide shooting also belongs to these traditions.

108877.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society. The municipality makes careful considerations when granting festival permits. Excessive noise nuisance must be prevented.

108880.explanation: The CDA wants it to be possible for everyone to participate in society. If this is financially impossible, there are additional provisions available, not only for people on social assistance.

108881.explanation: The local broadcaster receives a contribution from the municipality. Nationally, a proposal is upcoming for extra money to local broadcasters from the national government.

108883.explanation: The CDA wants it to be possible for everyone to participate in society. If this is financially impossible, there are additional provisions available.

108884.explanation: We encourage an active sporting, cultural, and welfare life so that residents can continue to participate. Associations are very important in this.

108885.explanation: It is important to preserve local heritage. That gives character and identity to our municipality.

108886.explanation: Safety is a prerequisite for a strong community. According to the CDA, more enforcement does not always mean more safety. We believe in the power of social cohesion, good accessibility of cooperating professionals, and a safe environment.

108887.explanation: CCTV can make crime detection more effective and faster. Since more and more private individuals have cameras, there is no immediate ambition for the municipality here, but it is certainly open for discussion.

108888.explanation: Victims of domestic violence deserve the help and support they need, also in our municipality. We must keep looking for possibilities to temporarily offer safety even within our own municipality.

108890.explanation: The construction of affordable homes for the elderly can stimulate housing market flow. Therefore, it is important to build enough suitable homes for seniors.

108891.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended for permanent residence.

108892.explanation: The CDA supports 'one more street' and 'one more neighborhood.' This approach ensures livability in every village and, according to Bouwend Nederland, offers the best chance for quickly increasing housing.

108893.explanation: If circumstances allow, this should certainly be encouraged. We are happiest when we live in community with others, when we are seen and valued. Obligatory socially valuable work goes too far.

108895.explanation: The CDA finds it unacceptable that the municipality you live in determines to what extent you can make ends meet. Because local charges can also differ and vulnerable people deserve support, we must develop appropriate policy here.

108897.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

108899.explanation: The CDA values choice options in healthcare. Sometimes the differences in rates are so large or the quality so low that it is undesirable for every care provider to be funded by the municipality.

108900.explanation: Good youth care is priceless. Naturally, costs should be considered, but the quality of service must be paramount.

108904.explanation: The CDA stands for a fair and strong economy. For this, it is important that there remains room for new business activity in our municipality. Preference goes to expanding existing business parks.

108906.explanation: Every municipality is obliged to shelter a number of status holders. Even if priority ends, our municipality retains this legal obligation. With the current housing shortage, priority for status holders can be better balanced against other groups who also have priority for housing.

108907.explanation: We respect the methodology of the solar ladder, where solar panels on roofs are preferred. We want to preserve our valuable agricultural land as much as possible for food production.

108908.explanation: The CDA is in favor of more facilities. However, there is a private initiative underway, and we will wait for that.

108909.explanation: Swimming skills of our children are important. The CDA is open to initiatives for school swimming, but this primarily lies with the schools.

108910.explanation: Primary schools often pay attention to dialect. Often as part of a broad approach strategy to recognize and utilize multilingualism. The schools set their own policy on this.

108911.explanation: Coffeeshops generally attract new criminal activity. The CDA prefers to reduce the number of coffeeshops rather than increase them.

108912.explanation: The Housing Act mandates that 30% of new construction must be social housing. The local CDA wants to comply with the laws. The CDA supports a good mix of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes across different segments.

108916.explanation: At the regional level, we must reach good agreements within the framework of the energy transition. We prefer solar panels on roofs and (clean) sustainable energy projects at the local level.

108917.explanation: The CDA believes it must remain possible for residents to make physical appointments and that the municipal office door remains open for everyone. It is important to approach citizens who need to contact the municipality carefully and respectfully and to handle their requests for assistance properly.

108865.explanation: It is about the quality of the administrators.

108866.explanation: Consultation does take place; however, decision-making remains with the municipal government.

108868.explanation: The municipality of Alphen-Chaam is already expensive compared to neighboring municipalities.

108870.explanation: We already comply with the distribution law; accommodation for Ukrainians is already under construction.

108871.explanation: This is not always possible, and we urgently need these employees.

108873.explanation: Provided that no nuisance is caused to neighbors.

108874.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for sustainable business operations.

108875.explanation: Due to the national ban, we see no need for a municipal alternative. It is also costly given the three centers are spread apart.

108877.explanation: Noise standards do not need to be stricter, but the sound may not be louder than currently allowed. Furthermore, we no longer want to permit beer carts with extremely loud and low bass sounds.

108881.explanation: We do not find extra funding for the local broadcaster necessary. We stand for a small government.

108883.explanation: Free choice. We do not see learning to play a musical instrument as basic development for children.

108884.explanation: In general, sports clubs have sufficient means to generate enough income through membership fees, special actions, etc. In extreme situations, we are open to thinking along with solutions.

108885.explanation: Monument care is part of culture and heritage. It is one of the core points of the Cittaslow philosophy. We do not want to cut back on this.

108886.explanation: The deployment and visibility of enforcement officers and supervisors is an important task of the municipality. In Alphen-Chaam, this must improve.

108888.explanation: Not applicable to our municipality. We find it important that victims are helped safely, but this must be organized smartly and regionally. Accommodation in a small municipality is inefficient.

108890.explanation: Alphen-Chaam needs smaller owner-occupied homes with gardens for the elderly.

108891.explanation: With the restriction that a small part of the holiday park may be permanently inhabited, thus not harming tourism.

108892.explanation: The municipality must allow new construction outside existing village boundaries in certain areas.

108897.explanation: The municipality must adapt to the electrification of cars.

108900.explanation: It is about the price-quality ratio, not just price.

108904.explanation: Entrepreneurs in Alphen-Chaam request this, and we want to bind them to us.

108907.explanation: Does not align with the Cittaslow philosophy.

108908.explanation: 't Zand must always remain freely accessible.

108909.explanation: Given the financial situation of the municipality of Alphen-Chaam for the next 5 years, we place this responsibility with the parents. This is a responsible choice for our municipality.

108910.explanation: We do not see subsidizing specific dialects as a municipal task. Schools can make their own choices about this.

108911.explanation: Not applicable to our municipality. We rely on larger surrounding municipalities; we are too small a municipality to facilitate this ourselves.

108912.explanation: In Alphen-Chaam there is more need for owner-occupied homes compared to the national average.

108916.explanation: Due to the low-flying zone for the Gilze-Rijen air base, there is no room for wind turbines in Alphen-Chaam.

108917.explanation: We are a large municipality. It is important for residents of all centers to be able to ask questions about municipal matters easily.

108865.explanation: D66 strives for a reflection of our society in public administration.

108866.explanation: It is good that our residents are involved in important decisions. D66 wants residents to be able to propose decisions made by the municipal council for a corrective referendum.

108868.explanation: Permits are not a cash cow. However, the cost coverage of the work required to obtain a permit must be taken into account. If these costs increase, then the price of the permit will also increase.

108870.explanation: Alphen-Chaam collaborates with the municipalities in Hart van Brabant to accommodate refugees. Alphen-Chaam takes responsibility for housing a portion of the Ukrainian refugees.

108871.explanation: The time has passed when employers shifted housing responsibilities onto society. Employers must therefore take responsibility for the housing of seasonal workers.

108873.explanation: Research shows that wood burning has a significant negative impact on clean air. Alphen-Chaam participates in the Clean Air Agreement, which aligns with this approach.

108874.explanation: The use of outdoor terrace heaters continues to grow. D66 wants to stop heating the outdoor air. This is good for sustainability and responds to the government's and energy suppliers' call to reduce electricity consumption in winter.

108875.explanation: D66 agrees with the Dutch ban on fireworks. On the other hand, it is good to enjoy fireworks together and wish each other well. Let's do that with animal-friendly fireworks.

108877.explanation: Noise standards in Alphen-Chaam are acceptable in themselves. However, D66 asks for more attention to enforcement of these standards, particularly regarding sound vehicles inside and outside the carnival season.

108880.explanation: Sports are good for relaxation and health. It is important that they are accessible to everyone in the municipality.

108881.explanation: Local democracy also includes local media. D66 believes local media, especially in investigative journalism, should receive extra support.

108883.explanation: D66 values that every child has access to sports and culture. Therefore, it is good that the municipal website highlights financial support available to less affluent groups.

108884.explanation: The entire village community benefits from a vital association life. The municipality has a task to support this, including strengthening associations and supporting meeting places and neighborhood initiatives.

108885.explanation: We must take good care of our monuments. This includes maintenance. Additionally, D66 supports initiatives to rebuild lost monuments, such as the Weltevreden mill.

108886.explanation: We collaborate well with Baarle and Gilze and Rijen in the field of safety. Especially enforcement in rural areas and nature should be intensified.

108887.explanation: We have enough cameras in our municipality in the right places. We believe that adding more cameras would excessively infringe on our residents' privacy.

108888.explanation: We cooperate regionally in Hart van Brabant for healthcare. Additionally, D66 believes more attention should be given to partner violence.

108890.explanation: Alphen-Chaam is aging more and more, and there are increasing numbers of single-person households. It is good to build for these needs, not just smaller homes. At the same time, more multigenerational homes are needed, among other reasons to support informal caregiving.

108891.explanation: In Alphen-Chaam there are parks where people have had a permanent residence for years. D66 believes this should be legalized for them.

108892.explanation: D66 wants careful use of all space, also outside the village boundaries. At the same time, D66 wants to meet housing demand. If a quality opportunity arises outside the existing village boundaries, we should seize it.

108893.explanation: We need volunteers based on willingness, not coercion, also for our association life. By connecting these tasks, we create a win-win situation: social participation and a stronger association life.

108895.explanation: The income limit is currently set at 110%. This is well below the Dutch average. It is time to raise this limit to an acceptable level of 120%.

108897.explanation: Not everyone has their own driveway. The transition to electric driving will continue steadily in the coming years. It is therefore good to expand the possibilities for public charging.

108899.explanation: Our healthcare providers are neutral. We ask them to care for everyone with the same respect for religion, culture, or beliefs.

108900.explanation: Focusing only on price in healthcare demonstrably does not work. On the contrary: focusing on quality of care leads to cheaper care. Additionally, focusing mainly on price can result in longer waiting lists. D66 finds this unacceptable, especially regarding youth care.

108904.explanation: D66 believes that first better use should be made of the current business parks.

108906.explanation: People with a residence permit are assigned to our municipality for housing. Therefore, we target a percentage of 15% for refugees, urgent cases, and exiting protected living.

108907.explanation: D66 wants entrepreneurs in our rural area to have perspective. This includes providing (financial) perspective. Installing solar panels can contribute to that. Moreover, this supports the energy transition and nature development.

108908.explanation: D66 believes that closing this location should not have happened. Additionally, this location has deteriorated for years and plans have stalled. It is time to restore the natural outdoor pool!

108909.explanation: Not everyone is able to enroll their children in swimming lessons. We must be aware of that and provide a solution. Currently, there is a scheme for this. If it proves insufficient, the municipality must step in.

108910.explanation: D66 warmly supports the uniqueness of our villages and the local history association as guardian of the Allefs dialect. Mandatory attention to the dialect in schools is a step too far for D66: math, the Dutch language, and citizenship have priority.

108911.explanation: D66 finds it important that the legalization of soft drugs remains on the agenda. We value health and legalization contributes to that. By the way, an entrepreneur will sooner be established in Breda or Tilburg than in Alphen or Chaam.

108912.explanation: This agreement is necessary and has been made together. Let’s now maintain this agreement for the long term! Good for our residents and clear for our developers.

108916.explanation: For a stable electricity grid and a sustainable municipality, local generation of solar and wind energy is important. Therefore, D66 wants to realize this in suitable locations, such as along the A16.

108917.explanation: It is good to serve residents in their own village and to answer questions about municipal matters. For services like passports, IDs, or driving licenses, it can be better organized centrally. Therefore, D66 welcomes the municipality enabling the delivery of these products.

108865.explanation: We seek the right person for the right position. Ideally, this is a good mix of men and women, as in the current executive board.

108866.explanation: Residents come first with us. We listen to residents on important topics. Because experiences with referenda have been mixed, we find it important to think carefully together before using this instrument.

108868.explanation: Permit prices must be cost-covering. This is already the case. Prices do not need to be increased further.

108870.explanation: We are realizing two locations for housing Ukrainians, one in Chaam and one in Alphen, for a maximum of five years and within the municipal council’s framework. Further refugee accommodation is currently not planned.

108871.explanation: Housing seasonal workers is a task of the municipality. We also see that sometimes nuisance occurs. We will therefore need to have discussions about this, including the roles of employers and other governments.

108873.explanation: Clean air is essential. We want an air quality monitoring station and participate in the Clean Air Agreement. The focus is mainly on communication and education. Although fireplaces and wood stoves can cause nuisance, a ban is not currently on the table for us.

108874.explanation: Terrace heating is often done with gas. That is not sustainable. The use of electric heaters is more efficient and less environmentally harmful. Our focus is primarily on communication and education, and at this time not on a ban.

108875.explanation: A fireworks ban is coming. We believe an alternative is needed. As far as we're concerned, we offer room within permit issuance. Organization and costs should remain with private, locally supported initiatives. A municipal fireworks show sounds appealing, but the question is whether that is realistic.

108877.explanation: We have various events. Think of Carnival, the 8 of Chaam, the Galders Festival Weekend, truck and tractor pulling, and the car and motocross. These celebrations are characteristic of our villages and belong here. We want to keep it that way. Even stricter noise standards should not hinder this.

108880.explanation: Everyone matters and everyone should be able to participate. There are already various schemes to support residents who have financial difficulties. We want to raise awareness of these schemes and simplify the application process. This way, people can also join sports clubs.

108881.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives financial support. Additional financial support is not currently on the agenda for us.

108883.explanation: Culture connects and gives meaning. We enable music lessons by supporting associations, the Youth Sports and Culture Fund, and through schemes that assist residents with low income in participating in sports, cultural and social activities. We do not see the need for extra subsidies.

108884.explanation: Exercise and sports are part of a healthy foundation. We support sports clubs with subsidies. With the football clubs, we examine whether the current accommodations still fit.

108885.explanation: We are proud of the historic character of our villages. We want to preserve that. This is currently possible with the limited funds available for monument care. This ensures that our monuments are adequately protected and maintained. Budget cuts are therefore not an option.

108886.explanation: As a first step, we look at where residents experience the most nuisance and insecurity. Enforcement efforts should focus there. Then we assess whether more enforcement officers are needed.

108887.explanation: If safety issues justify it, we include camera surveillance in our considerations. This is the case at recreation lake 't Zand, where camera surveillance is already present.

108888.explanation: This is an important theme that we tackle regionally together. Our municipality is too small to manage this on its own.

108890.explanation: We focus on better flow. We especially want to build affordable homes for (single) seniors and young people, both for rent and purchase. We first build for our own residents. Where legally possible, we give (former) residents priority.

108891.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday homes is undesirable. If we cooperate with conversion to housing, it will only be at non-vital recreation parks and under conditions agreed upon.

108892.explanation: We prioritize building plans in the right locations. Within four years, we aim to start building 200 homes. New plans without clear added value receive less priority. New construction is preferably within village boundaries, but outside them is also open for discussion if opportunities arise.

108893.explanation: This benefit is not optional. We want residents to participate and be part of society. For some, this is a path to work; for others, having meaningful daytime activities and a healthy rhythm. Socially meaningful work is a good means for this.

108895.explanation: We consider this a challenge that should be addressed at the national level. We do not think it is the municipality’s role to arrange this.

108897.explanation: This is not necessary at the moment because there is sufficient supply. Our principle is to align supply with demand.

108899.explanation: The municipality has agreements with a certain number of care providers. We do this to monitor quality, agree on rates, and prevent care funds being used by providers for non-care purposes. This also means that residents do not always have full free choice on all fronts.

108900.explanation: We want to move towards a funding system that keeps costs as much as possible under control. But we also look beyond price alone. We also consider aspects such as the provider’s quality and experience and system simplification.

108904.explanation: There are sufficient business parks. If there is demand for expansion, we first look at optimal use and infill on the current sites.

108906.explanation: We see this as a regional task because our municipality is too small to manage this alone. Especially given the existing housing needs of our residents.

108907.explanation: There is a major challenge regarding the energy transition. In Alphen-Chaam, particularly opportunities for green gas exist. We focus on that.

108908.explanation: We respect past agreements that provide a good balance between wellness and nature. This combination boosts landscape and nature development, the recreational value of the area, and the region’s tourist economy. Swimming is possible at 't Zand.

108909.explanation: School swimming contributes to safety and encourages exercise. Although a nice idea, we consider this a challenge that should be tackled at the national level. Our municipality is too small to manage this.

108910.explanation: Preserving cultural and historical heritage is important to us. The local dialects of all our villages fall under this as well. We mainly see a role for our historical societies in this.

108911.explanation: There is sufficient availability of coffeeshops in nearby cities, which also have the knowledge and experience to handle soft drugs policy. We consider our municipality too small to regulate this ourselves.

108912.explanation: We follow the regional housing deal. This guides us on affordability, seniors, starters, and other target groups. In new projects, the 30-40-30 rule is guiding: 30% social rental, 40% mid-rent/mid-buy, and 30% free sector.

108916.explanation: There is a major challenge regarding the energy transition. In Alphen-Chaam, particularly opportunities for green gas exist. We focus on that.

108917.explanation: The municipality must be service-oriented. That is why a municipal desk remains in our municipality, but not in every village. That is too expensive. Those who can’t go to the desk receive an alternative (e.g., delivery). We expand opening hours so residents can come outside office hours.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to ensure that no wind turbines are built in Alphen‑Chaam.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet er bij de provincie op aandringen dat er geen

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Alphen-Chaam should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Alphen-Chaam moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes for seniors should be built in Alphen-Chaam.
There should be more camera surveillance in Alphen-Chaam.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: More business parks should be created in Alphen-Chaam.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Allefs dialect.
A women's shelter should be established in Alphen-Chaam.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Alphen-Chaam.

titleParts.text: In Alphen-Chaam moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety should be employed in Alphen-Chaam.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The prices of municipal permits must be raised.

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Alphen-Chaam should be social housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Alphen-Chaam moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Each village in the municipality should have a municipal service desk where residents can go with questions about municipal matters.

shootoutStatements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

shootoutStatements.theme: New plus sports park / cultural center / swimming pool
shootoutStatements.title: Alphen-Chaam must build a new swimming pool on Alphensebaan.
